calendar.matters:
Amended Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration In Support Of Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kathy L. Broussard, in pro per, Tel. 916.617.9316, for plaintiffs;
Lisa Berkowitz, Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3825, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER KATHY BROUSSARD Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is denied. Plaintiff fails to provide a memorandum of points and authorities in compliance with CRC 3.1112 that supports the instant motion. Upon reviewing plaintiff's Second Amended Petition, the gist of plaintiff's claim is that defendant erred because it was unaware of information from Mr. Wolfgang and Mr. Dayce. The record belies this assertion. Ms. Levy interviewed those witnesses (AR 00654, 00657, 00886, 00892.) In addition, defendant did not conduct the investigation; it merely relied on the information that was presented to it. In sum, plaintiff fails to show that the weight of the evidence does not support defendant's decision. (Bixby v. Pierno (1971) 4 Cal.3d 130, 143; Angelier v. State Board of Pharmacy (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 592, 597.). Order and judgment signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Kathy Broussard's motion for leave to file a second amended petition for writ of administrative mandamus is granted. The City filed a statement of non-opposition to the motion. Ms. Broussard is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

Amended Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration In Support Of Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Continued To Dec-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - KATHY L. BROUSSARD, TEL. NO. 1-916-617-9316, PETITIONER APPEARING IN PRO-PER;
ATTY. LISA BERKOWITZ, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3825 APPEARING FOR THE RESPODENT.

RULING - The matter was discussed whether the City has been properly served with the motion to file a second amended petition or that counsel representing the City is even aware that such a motion was filed because if Petitioner Kathy Broussard's motion for leave to file a second amended petition, is granted, would moot her request that her first amended petition be granted. The court ruled as follows: PETITIONER KATHY BROUSSARD'S AMENDED Motion in support of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued to December 7, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. If the City decides to oppose the motion to file the Second Amended Petition, Opposition shall be filed by November 15, 2016; Reply to be filed by November 30, 2016. If by November 15 and the City decides not to oppose the Motion For Leave to file the Second Amended Petition, the parties need to confer with a briefing schedule regarding the hearing on the Second Amended Petition. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
